standard: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
studentFriendlySkill: Ask and answer questions about important story details.
teacherUse: Use when students need to identify key details before writing from a story.

standard: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
studentFriendlySkill: Describe how characters respond to important events.
teacherUse: Use when the reading passage has a clear character response, challenge, or problem.

standard: Write narratives in which they recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events, include details, and provide a sense of closure.
studentFriendlySkill: Write a story with events, details, and an ending.
teacherUse: Use for short story writing, chapter writing, and narrative responses.

standard: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
studentFriendlySkill: Use clues to understand new words.
teacherUse: Use when a passage includes vocabulary that supports visual word study.

standard: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
studentFriendlySkill: Answer questions using evidence from the text.
teacherUse: Use when students need to support reading-to-writing ideas with text evidence.

standard: Describe characters in a story and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
studentFriendlySkill: Describe characters and how their actions move the story forward.
teacherUse: Use for passages with character action, plot movement, and scene writing.

standard: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
studentFriendlySkill: Write a story with clear events, details, and sequence.
teacherUse: Use for story scenes, class movie scenes, or chapter writing.

standard: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details.
studentFriendlySkill: Tell a story using clear and helpful details.
teacherUse: Use when students will present scenes or read their writing aloud.

standard: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text.
studentFriendlySkill: Describe characters, settings, and important events using details from the text.
teacherUse: Use when the reading passage has clear characters, setting, and event sequence.

standard: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
studentFriendlySkill: Write a story with clear events, descriptive details, and sequence.
teacherUse: Use when students are writing stories, scenes, chapters, or narrative responses.

standard: With guidance and support, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
studentFriendlySkill: Plan, revise, and improve your writing.
teacherUse: Use when students will revise scenes after AI or teacher feedback.

standard: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
studentFriendlySkill: Use context to understand new words and phrases.
teacherUse: Use when extracting visual vocabulary from a reading passage.

standard: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text and summarize the text.
studentFriendlySkill: Find the theme and summarize the story.
teacherUse: Use when a passage has a clear lesson, message, or theme.

standard: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama.
studentFriendlySkill: Compare characters, settings, or events.
teacherUse: Use when students compare scene choices or character reactions.

standard: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
studentFriendlySkill: Write a story with technique, details, and clear sequence.
teacherUse: Use for class book chapters, movie scenes, or narrative extensions.

standard: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details.
studentFriendlySkill: Present ideas in a logical order with strong details.
teacherUse: Use when students present their class book or movie scenes.

standard: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text.
studentFriendlySkill: Find a theme or central idea and summarize it with details.
teacherUse: Use for upper elementary or middle school reading-to-writing lessons.

standard: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change.
studentFriendlySkill: Describe plot episodes and how characters change.
teacherUse: Use for scene-based writing where students build plot and character change.

standard: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
studentFriendlySkill: Write a well-structured story with details and clear events.
teacherUse: Use for class movie scenes and longer narrative projects.

standard: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
studentFriendlySkill: Use context and word parts to understand new vocabulary.
teacherUse: Use when the text includes words worth turning into visual vocabulary cards.
